title: Who Knew Interstate Camping Could Be So Nice?
summary:
Our next stop after leaving Tucson was the Eastern Sierra, but we didn't want to do the whole ten hour drive in one push. Our van doesn't have cruise control and we have become moderate to severe weenies when it comes to spending long days on the road. We decided to spend the night at Chiriaco Summit. Despite being within eye and earshot of I-10, it was a pretty nice spot to stay for the night, with free camping and plenty of empty spots. We made a salad for dinner and watched the new Netflix series Big Mistakes. On the way out, we got some great coffee at the little shop at the rest area near where we camped.

-Colin

photos.caption: The sunset view from our campsite at Chiriaco Summit.
